warnings: logP from semi-empirical ΔG_solv differences is screening-grade; ±1 log unit typical.

Each leg is an electronic-energy difference on a single frozen geometry — no ZPE/thermal/entropy and no per-phase relaxation. The reported delta_G_solv_* legs are electronic only; the 1 atm→1 M standard-state term cancels in the water−octanol difference, so logP itself is insensitive to it, but the individual legs are not corrected ΔG*_solv.
For chemoinformatic logP, also consider RDKit's Crippen/XLogP (group-contribution).

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Method provenance:
Property: octanol–water logP (dimensionless partition coefficient)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) implemented in xtb  (density-fit by construction; no Gaussian basis)
Geometry provenance: fixed geometry taken directly from the user-supplied file /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/logp-partition-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no further optimisation (single-conformer logP = logP//geometry)
Solvent model: implicit ALPB/GBSA as implemented in xtb for water and for octanol; gas-phase reference explicitly noted; no explicit solvent molecules
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (neutral singlet)
Software: chemkit logp-partition skill → xtb backend (version not reported in JSON)
Convergence & caveats: SCF steps converged for each leg; see warnings echoed above. Single-conformer result, no Boltzmann averaging.

Reproducibility: command recorded in result JSON (see cli_invocation), output JSON written to aniline_logp_xtb.json; live engine stdout/stderr were streamed to the default logp_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (tail ‑f possible during run).
